concerns.text: Task T011 implements sensitivity analysis for thresholds `T ∈ {, 500, 600}`, omitting the first value `400` required by spec.md FR-007 and US-3. The task description explicitly lists an empty set member, failing to cover the full requirement scope.

Task T006a instructs the implementation to use a nonsensical placeholder string ('The ratio of the planetary radius...') as the final code formula. This contradicts spec.md FR-012 which mandates the specific mathematical formula `6371 / Vsw_mean`. The task fails to provide a valid implementation path for the requirement.

Tasks T008 and T009 contain unexpanded placeholder text ('A sufficient number of iterations...') instead of the concrete iteration counts (10,000 and 1,000) mandated by spec.md FR-005 and FR-006. Without these specific values, the tasks cannot satisfy the testability and reproducibility requirements of the spec.

Phase 7 tasks (T045-T050) implement Alfvén speed calculations, reference frame transformations, and schematic diagrams. These capabilities are not authorized by spec.md (which limits scope to linear correlation, permutation tests, and a fixed heuristic threshold) and contradict the 'Scope Alignment' note in tasks.md itself. These tasks represent unauthorized scope creep and fail to map to any existing FR/SC.

Task T020 is marked as 'REJECTED' and blocked due to missing files, yet the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section claims Phase 3 can start after Foundational completion. This creates a deadlock where the critical path for User Story 1 (US-1) is blocked by a task that cannot be completed, leaving the US-1 Independent Test (FR-005/FR-006 integration) without a valid implementation task.

The independent test description for User Story 2 (Phase 4) contains a placeholder ('The true lag is approximately one hour.') instead of the specific synthetic dataset condition defined in spec.md US-2 (true lag of 45 min). This prevents the task from satisfying the specific acceptance scenario required by the spec.

Task T002 description contains the phrase 'Initialize Python latest stable project' which is vague and contradicts the specific pinned versions listed in the requirements block. While the versions are present, the task description itself is imprecise.
